name: SYNB1618 phenylketonuria first-in-human phase 1/2a study
summary: Randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind, multicenter inpatient phase 1/2a study of engineered E. coli Nissle 1917 strain SYNB1618 in healthy adult volunteers and adults with phenylketonuria.
population: Healthy adult volunteers and adults with phenylketonuria and blood phenylalanine at least 600 micromol/L

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for Live Biotherapeutics and Engineered Microbes because they can show whether the intervention changes safety, biomarkers, and mechanistic endpoints in Healthy adult volunteers and adults with phenylketonuria and blood phenylalanine at least 600 micromol/L. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
